document.title: Image Quality and Radiation Dose on Digital Chest Imaging: Comparison of Amorphous Silicon and Amorphous Selenium Flat-Panel Systems
document.documentType: Journal article; chest imaging original research
standards: European Guidelines on Quality Criteria for Diagnostic Radiographic Images
ICRP publication 60: 1990 recommendations of the International Commission on Radiological Protection

sourceSummary.oneLine: AJR 2006 article comparing image quality and radiation dose for amorphous silicon and amorphous selenium flat-panel detector systems in digital chest imaging.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Literature evidence for clinical validation and verification of digital flat-panel X-ray detector performance; not an authority-issued regulatory approval document.
keyPoints: The study compared chest imaging using amorphous silicon and amorphous selenium flat-panel detector systems.
Two groups of 100 patients each had digital chest radiographs acquired with either detector system.
Image quality was assessed by five experienced radiologists using the European Guidelines on Quality Criteria for Diagnostic Radiographic Images.
The amorphous silicon system produced significantly lower effective dose than the amorphous selenium system for posteroanterior and lateral views.

The conclusion states that chest radiographs can be acquired with significantly lower patient radiation dose using an amorphous silicon flat-panel system while producing equal or superior image quality.

concepts.name: Amorphous silicon flat-panel detector
concepts.evidence: The amorphous silicon flat-panel detector system (Vertix FD, Siemens Medical Solutions; and Pixium 4600, Trixell)

concepts.name: Amorphous selenium flat-panel detector
concepts.evidence: The amorphous selenium flat-panel detector (Epex, Hologic; and DirectRay DR 1000, Direct Radiography Corporation)

concepts.name: Digital chest radiography
concepts.evidence: The aim of this study was to compare the image quality and radiation dose in chest imaging using an amorphous silicon flat-panel detector system and an amorphous selenium flat-panel detector system.

concepts.name: Effective dose
concepts.evidence: The effective dose (E), representative of the risk of late radiation-induced effects such as malignancies

concepts.name: European Guidelines on Quality Criteria for Diagnostic Radiographic Images
concepts.pageTarget: European Guidelines on Quality Criteria for Diagnostic Radiographic Images
concepts.evidence: image quality using a method adopted from the European Guidelines on Quality Criteria for Diagnostic Radiographic Images

concepts.name: Contrast-detail phantom study
concepts.evidence: a contrast–detail phantom study (CDRAD 2.0, Artinis Medical Systems) was set up

evidence.claim: The article was published in AJR volume 187 in September 2006.
evidence.pageTarget: Image Quality and Radiation Dose on Digital Chest Imaging

evidence.claim: The study objective was to compare image quality and radiation dose in chest imaging between amorphous silicon and amorphous selenium flat-panel detector systems.
evidence.evidenceText: The aim of this study was to compare the image quality and radiation dose in chest imaging using an amorphous silicon flat-panel detector system and an amorphous selenium flat-panel detector system.

evidence.claim: The patient study used two groups of 100 patients each.
evidence.evidenceText: In two groups of 100 patients each, digital chest radiographs were acquired with either an amorphous silicon or an amorphous selenium flat-panel system.
evidence.pageTarget: Clinical evaluation of flat-panel detector systems

evidence.claim: The amorphous silicon system included Vertix FD and Pixium 4600.
evidence.evidenceText: The amorphous silicon flat-panel detector system (Vertix FD, Siemens Medical Solutions; and Pixium 4600, Trixell)

evidence.claim: The amorphous selenium system included Epex and DirectRay DR 1000.
evidence.evidenceText: The amorphous selenium flat-panel detector (Epex, Hologic; and DirectRay DR 1000, Direct Radiography Corporation)

evidence.claim: Clinical image quality was assessed by five experienced chest radiologists using European quality criteria.
evidence.evidenceText: Five experienced chest radiologists assessed all soft-copy posteroanterior chest radiographs and scored the image quality using a method adopted from the European Guidelines on Quality Criteria for Diagnostic Radiographic Images.
evidence.pageTarget: European Guidelines on Quality Criteria for Diagnostic Radiographic Images

evidence.claim: For posteroanterior view, effective dose was 9.6 μSv for amorphous silicon and 22.6 μSv for amorphous selenium.
evidence.evidenceText: For the posteroanterior view, the effective doses were 9.6 and 22.6 μSv for the amorphous silicon and amorphous selenium systems, respectively.
evidence.pageTarget: Radiation dose in digital chest radiography

evidence.claim: For lateral view, effective dose was 27.1 μSv for amorphous silicon and 79.2 μSv for amorphous selenium.
evidence.evidenceText: For the lateral view, effective doses of 27.1 μSv (amorphous silicon) and 79.2 μSv (amorphous selenium) were obtained.
evidence.pageTarget: Radiation dose in digital chest radiography

evidence.claim: The amorphous silicon system showed significantly better low-contrast performance for phantom entrance doses up to 135 μGy.
evidence.evidenceText: Overall, the contrast–detail study showed a significantly better low-contrast performance of the amorphous silicon system compared with the amorphous selenium detector system for phantom entrance doses up to 135 μGy (p < 0.032).

evidence.claim: The article concluded that amorphous silicon flat-panel chest radiographs can be acquired at significantly lower patient radiation dose while producing equal or superior image quality.
evidence.evidenceText: Chest radiographs can be acquired with a significantly lower patient radiation dose using an amorphous silicon flat-panel system than using an amorphous selenium flat-panel system, thereby producing images that are equal or even superior in quality to those of the amorphous selenium flat-panel detector system.

reviewFlags.reason: No regulatory authority, approval status, certificate number, or jurisdictional authorization is present; this is a journal literature source.
No document revision or version is stated in the source text.
The source metadata productSignal is Unclassified, while the article discusses several detector systems and component models.
